title: Built-In Style Bookcases
summary: A pair of plywood bookcases with adjustable shelf-pin shelving and simple frame-and-panel doors, braced into the wall to look built in.
description:
A freestanding bookcase reads as furniture; this one is built and installed to read as part of the room. The trick isn't fancy joinery — it's a column of shelf-pin holes for adjustable shelving, trim on only the faces that show, and a brace back to the wall studs so the case can't tip or shift once it's loaded with books.

**Skip trim on any side that will sit flush against a wall.** Trim there does nothing but add a gap you'd have to caulk, since nobody will ever see that edge once the case is in place.

The doors are a simple frame with a groove routed into the inside edge to hold a thin plywood panel — far simpler than a raised panel door, and appropriate for a piece that's meant to disappear into the room rather than draw attention to itself.

tools.note: Or a table saw for breaking down the plywood.
Also drills the shelf-pin hole columns.
Cuts the groove that holds each door's plywood panel.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Box sides, top, bottom, fixed shelf, and adjustable shelves for both cases.

materials.name: Wall-mounting brackets and screws
materials.note: Braces each case to the wall studs so it can't tip.

steps.title: Build each box frame
steps.body: Build each bookcase's box frame — sides, top, bottom, and a fixed shelf that also serves as structure — from 3/4" plywood, joined with pocket screws and glue.

steps.title: Drill the shelf-pin columns
steps.body: Drill an evenly spaced column of shelf-pin holes on the inside front and back of each side panel, so the shelves can be repositioned later instead of fixed.

steps.title: Cut the adjustable shelves
steps.body: Cut the adjustable shelves from 3/4" plywood to fit each case, then sand them.

steps.title: Build the doors
steps.body: Assemble a simple frame for each door, rout a groove along the inside edge, and glue a 1/4" plywood panel into the groove.

steps.title: Trim and finish
steps.body: Add trim across the front and any exposed side of each case, filling nail holes — skip trim on any side that will sit flush against a wall. Paint every component before installation.

steps.title: Install
steps.body: Remove any base moulding along the back and side walls where each case will sit, slide the case into place, and brace it to the wall studs so it can't tip or shift. Hang the doors and drop the shelves onto the shelf pins.
